Source_Species.reason: PEN3 is studied in Arabidopsis lines in this paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The plant material throughout is Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Col-0 is the wild-type background used.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PEN3 is referred to as defense cargo, but 'ABC transporter' is not stated in this paper's Results.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: PEN3 is described as focal accumulating at fungal penetration sites, and prior proteomics cited in Results identified PEN3 as cargo of SYP61-SVs; plasma membrane localization is not newly shown here but is consistent with the marker line context.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper explicitly uses the published transgenic line pPEN3:PEN3-GFP.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show reduced PEN3-GFP focal accumulation intensity in ipcs2W205* and genetic analysis indicates IPCS2 is in the PEN2/PEN3 pathway rather than PEN1.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are directly reported: double-mutant genetics, pPEN3:PEN3-GFP imaging, time-course CLSM quantification.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: PEN3 analyses were performed during Bgh infection.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper does not demonstrate direct physical interaction of PEN3 with IPCS2 or SYP61; only pathway association/cargo inference is discussed.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three listed findings are stated in Results: PEN3 focal accumulation at Bgh appressoria, unchanged frequency/area but reduced intensity in ipcs2W205*, and pathway placement with PEN2/PEN3.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This accurately reflects PEN3's role in this paper as a trafficking/defense readout rather than a nutrient metabolism enzyme.

corrections.reason: The paper does not demonstrate direct physical interaction of PEN3 with IPCS2 or SYP61; only pathway association/cargo inference is discussed.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: CLSM of the pSYP61:SYP61-CFP line is directly reported.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: SYP61 localization was examined under Bgh infection.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: No direct protein-protein interactions with IPCS2 or PEN3 are shown in this paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed relies mainly on localization plus prior proteomics to infer function.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly connect IPCS2 to IPC production and downstream GIPC classes, with IPC and Hex-GIPC reduced in ipcs2W205*.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: IPC/GIPCs are complex sphingolipids, explicitly discussed in Results.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The Summary and Results explicitly place IPCS2 in complex sphingolipid/GIPC biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Figure 2A and text show IPCS2 acts in the ceramide-to-IPC/GIPC branch.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The full gene/enzyme name is explicit in the title and text.

EC_Number.reason: The specific EC number 2.7.8.27 does not appear in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper identifies IPCS2 as an IPCS and discusses conserved catalytic motifs; phosphatidylinositol-to-ceramide headgroup transfer activity is described.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Ceramide is directly indicated as substrate; PI is stated as the source of the headgroup transferred by IPCSs.

Primary_Product.reason: Results state IPCSs produce the intermediate IPC.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Figure 2A/text describe transfer of a headgroup derived from PI to ceramides to produce IPC, enabling GIPC synthesis.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Results depict IPCS2 as the entry step into IPC/GIPC production from ceramide.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: IPC is shown as the immediate intermediate leading to GIPC production.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The Summary and Results state IPCS2 is at the TGN and previously co-localized with the SYP61-SV subdomain of the TGN.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state IPCS2 is the most highly expressed IPCS isoform and is induced by Bgh infection in wild-type leaves.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: qPCR directly showed IPCS2 induction in leaves upon Bgh infection.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper explicitly mentions pUBQ10:IPCS2-mVenus and yeast expression under the GPD promoter.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: All parts are supported by Results: loss of IPCS2 increases ceramide and penetration, reduces IPC and Hex-GIPC, and reduces PEN3 focal accumulation intensity.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches are explicitly used in Results and Methods.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The title identifies IPCS2 as 'a new rate-limiting component' in pathogen entry control.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed infection conditions and growth context appear in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results support that IPCS2-derived complex sphingolipids are required for pathogen entry control and PEN3 polarized secretion.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper supports TGN-associated production/incorporation into secretory vesicles, but does not directly measure a specific accumulation site for terminal metabolites.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: No direct physical interactions of IPCS2 with PEN3 or SYP61 are demonstrated.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: These four points accurately summarize the Results.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable implication but is not explicitly stated as an application in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Loss phenotypes and lesion tradeoff are shown, and constitutive overexpression causing spontaneous cell death is stated in Discussion with supporting Figure S3B.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Each listed point is discussed in the paper: ipcs2-1 transcript/profile discrepancy, possible post-transcriptional stabilization upon infection, and specificity to non-adapted powdery mildews in this study.
